    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input din_serial,
    input din_valid,
    output reg [7:0]dout_parallel,
    output reg dout_valid
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="serial2parallel">
    <description>Serial-to-Parallel Conversion Circuit</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="din_serial">Serial input data (single bit).</input>
        <input id="din_valid">Validity signal for input data.</input>
        <output id="dout_parallel" width_description="[7:0]">Parallel output data (8 bits wide).</output>
        <output id="dout_valid">Validity signal for the output data.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        The module utilizes a 4-bit counter (cnt) to keep track of the number of serial input data bits received. Every eight din_serial input, dout_parallel will output. When all 8 serial data bits have been received, the valid output signal is set to 1, indicating that the parallel output data is valid. Otherwise, the valid output signal is set to 0, indicating that the parallel output data is not valid.
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.gvd:
module div_8bit (
    input wire clk,
    input wire rst,
    input wire [7:0] dividend,
    input wire [7:0] divisor,
    input wire sign,
    input wire opn_valid,
    output reg res_valid,
    input wire res_ready,
    output reg [15:0] result
);
endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="div_8bit">
    <description>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst">The reset signal to initialize or reset the module.</input>
        <input id="sign">1-bit indicates if the operation is signed (1) or unsigned (0).</input>
        <input id="dividend" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the dividend for division.</input>
        <input id="divisor" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input signal representing the divisor for division.</input>
        <input id="opn_valid">1-bit indicates that a valid operation request is present.</input>
        <input id="res_ready">1-bit indicates that the 16-bit output result has already been acknowledged.</input>
        <output id="res_valid">1-bit output signal indicating the result is valid and ready.</output>
        <output id="result" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit the output containing the remainder in the upper 8 bits and the quotient in the lower 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Implement a simplified radix-2 divider on 8-bit signed or unsigned integers. The module accepts a dividend and a divisor as inputs and provides a 16-bit result containing both the quotient and the remainder. The design supports both signed and unsigned division operations.</implementation>
    <parameter_description>
        <state name="IDLE" value="2'b00"/>
        <state name="DIVIDE" value="2'b01"/>
        <state name="DONE" value="2'b10"/>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="state" type="2-bit" initial_value="IDLE">State variable for controlling the division process.</reg>
        <reg id="count" type="3-bit" initial_value="0">Count variable for tracking the number of iterations.</reg>
        <reg id="remainder" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current remainder during division.</reg>
        <reg id="quotient" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the current quotient during division.</reg>
        <reg id="dividend_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the dividend input.</reg>
        <reg id="divisor_reg" type="8-bit" initial_value="0">Register to hold the divisor input.</reg>
        <wire id="sub_result" type="9-bit"/> // Result of subtraction
        <wire id="carry_out" type="1-bit"/> // Carry out from subtraction
        <always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
            if (rst) begin
                state <= IDLE;
                count <= 0;
                remainder <= 0;
                quotient <= 0;
                dividend_reg <= 0;
                divisor_reg <= 0;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    IDLE: begin
                        if (opn_valid && !res_valid) begin
                            dividend_reg <= dividend;
                            divisor_reg <= divisor;
                            remainder <= {1'b0, dividend}; // Shift dividend left by 1
                            state <= DIVIDE;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DIVIDE: begin
                        if (count == 8) begin
                            state <= DONE;
                        end else begin
                            // Perform subtraction
                            sub_result = remainder + {8'b0, ~divisor_reg} + 1; // Two's complement for subtraction
                            carry_out = sub_result[8]; // Check carry out
                            
                            // Update remainder and quotient
                            remainder <= {sub_result[7:0], carry_out};
                            quotient <= {quotient[6:0], carry_out};
                            count <= count + 1;
                        end
                    end
                    
                    DONE: begin
                        state <= IDLE;
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
    </logic_description>
    <task>
        <step>On receiving a positive edge of the clock, check if the reset signal is active. If so, reset all registers to their initial values.</step>
        <step>When opn_valid is high and res_valid is low, save the inputs dividend and divisor into respective registers.</step>
        <step>Initialize the shift register SR with the absolute value of the dividend shifted left by one bit.</step>
        <step>Set NEG_DIVISOR to the negated absolute value of the divisor.</step>
        <step>Start the division process by setting the counter cnt to 1 and start_cnt to 1.</step>
        <step>During the division process, if the counter cnt reaches 8, the division is complete. Clear cnt and start_cnt, and update the shift register SR with the final remainder and quotient.</step>
        <step>Otherwise, increment the counter cnt and update the shift register SR based on the subtraction result. Use a multiplexer to select the appropriate result based on the carry-out, and update SR by shifting left and inserting the carry-out.</step>
        <step>After completing the division process, set res_valid to indicate that the result is valid and ready. Manage res_valid based on the reset signal, the counter, and whether the result has been consumed. Reset res_valid if rst is high. Set res_valid to 1 when cnt reaches 8. Set res_valid to 0 when res_valid and res_ready are both high. Otherwise, keep res_valid unchanged.</step>
    </task>
</module>

<module id="width_8to16">
    <description>Data width conversion circuit that converts 8-bit data input to 16-bit data output. Generates valid_out and data_out signals based on input conditions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating the validity of the input data.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data to be converted.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating the validity of the output data.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[15:0]">16-bit output data resulting from the width conversion.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>When valid_in is true, if flag is false, store data_in in data_lock. If flag is true, concatenate data_lock and data_in to generate valid_out and data_out. Reset all values on reset signal.</implementation>
</module>

samples.gvd:
module pe (
    input clk,
    input rst,
    input [31:0] a,
    input [31:0] b,
    output reg [31:0] c
);

    always @(posedge clk or posedge rst) begin
        if (rst) begin
            c <= 32'b0;
        end else begin
            c <= c + a * b;
        end
    end

endmodule

<module id="traffic_light">
    <description>Implement a traffic light system with red, yellow, and green indicators, controlled by a pedestrian button. The traffic light changes states based on the countdown timer and the pedestrian request.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="pass_request">Pedestrian button press signal.</input>
        <output id="clock" width_description="[7:0]">Countdown timer value.</output>
        <output id="red">Red light output signal.</output>
        <output id="yellow">Yellow light output signal.</output>
        <output id="green">Green light output signal.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <state id="idle">Idle state.</state>
        <state id="s1_red">State 1 - Red light on.</state>
        <state id="s2_yellow">State 2 - Yellow light on.</state>
        <state id="s3_green">State 3 - Green light on.</state>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <register id="cnt" width="8" description="Internal counter for timing control." />
        <register id="state" width="2" description="Current state of the traffic light controller." />
        <register id="p_red" width="1" description="Previous red light state." />
        <register id="p_yellow" width="1" description="Previous yellow light state." />
        <register id="p_green" width="1" description="Previous green light state." />
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // State transition logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                state <= idle;
                p_red <= 0;
                p_yellow <= 0;
                p_green <= 0;
            end else begin
                case (state)
                    idle: begin
                        state <= s1_red;
                        p_red <= 0;
                        p_yellow <= 0;
                        p_green <= 0;
                    end
                    s1_red: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 1;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                            p_red <= 1;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                    s2_yellow: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s1_red;
                            p_red <= 1;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 1;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end
                    end
                    s3_green: begin
                        if (cnt == 3) begin
                            state <= s2_yellow;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 1;
                            p_green <= 0;
                        end else begin
                            state <= s3_green;
                            p_red <= 0;
                            p_yellow <= 0;
                            p_green <= 1;
                        end
                    end
                endcase
            end
        end
        
        // Counting logic
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                cnt <= 10;
            end else begin
                if (pass_request && green) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else if (!green && p_green) begin
                    cnt <= 60;
                end else if (!yellow && p_yellow) begin
                    cnt <= 5;
                end else if (!red && p_red) begin
                    cnt <= 10;
                end else begin
                    cnt <= cnt - 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                red <= 0;
                yellow <= 0;
                green <= 0;
            end else begin
                red <= p_red;
                yellow <= p_yellow;
                green <= p_green;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

module fsm #(
    parameter s0 = 3'b000,
    parameter s1 = 3'b001,
    parameter s2 = 3'b010,
    parameter s3 = 3'b011,
    parameter s4 = 3'b100,
    parameter s5 = 3'b101
) (
    input IN,
    output reg MATCH,
    input CLK,
    input RST
); 
    
    reg [2:0] ST_cr, ST_nr;
    
    always @(posedge CLK or posedge RST) begin
        if (RST) begin
            ST_cr <= s0;
        end else begin
            ST_cr <= ST_nr;
        end
    end
    
    always @(*) begin
        case (ST_cr)
            s0: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s1;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s0;
                end
            end
            
            s1: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s1;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s2;
                end
            end
            
            s2: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s1;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s3;
                end
            end
            
            s3: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s4;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s0;
                end
            end
            
            s4: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s5;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s2;
                end
            end
            
            s5: begin
                if (IN == 1) begin
                    ST_nr = s1;
                end else begin
                    ST_nr = s2;
                end
            end
            
            default: begin
                ST_nr = s0;
            end
        endcase
    end
    
    always @(*) begin
        if (RST) begin
            MATCH = 0;
        end else begin
            case (ST_cr)
                s0: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end else begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end
                end
                
                s1: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end else begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end
                end
                
                s2: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end else begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end
                end
                
                s3: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin

                    end
                end
                
                s4: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin
                        MATCH = 1;
                    end else begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end
                end
                
                s5: begin
                    if (IN == 1) begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end else begin
                        MATCH = 0;
                    end
                end
                
                default: begin
                    MATCH = 0;
                end
            endcase

<module id="fsm">
    <description>Mealy FSM detection circuit that detects the sequence '10011' in the input IN. Outputs MATCH=1 when the sequence is detected, and MATCH=0 otherwise. Supports continuous input and loops through the sequence.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="IN">Input signal to be detected.</input>
        <input id="CLK">Clock signal for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal to initialize the FSM.</input>
        <output id="MATCH">Output signal indicating whether the sequence has been matched.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge CLK or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                MATCH <= 0;
            end else begin
                case ({IN, MATCH})
                    2'b10: MATCH <= 0;
                    2'b11: MATCH <= 1;
                    default: MATCH <= 0;
                endcase
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="freq_div">
    <description>Frequency divider for generating 50MHz, 10MHz, and 1MHz clocks from a 100MHz input clock.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="CLK_in">Input clock signal (100MHz).</input>
        <input id="RST">Reset signal (active low).</input>
        <output id="CLK_50">Output clock signal (50MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_10">Output clock signal (10MHz).</output>
        <output id="CLK_1">Output clock signal (1MHz).</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Counter for 50MHz generation
        reg [1:0] cnt_50;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_50 <= 2'b00;
            end else begin
                cnt_50 <= cnt_50 + 1;
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for 10MHz generation
        reg [3:0] cnt_10;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_10 <= 4'b0000;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_10 == 4'd4) begin
                    cnt_10 <= 4'b0000;
                end else begin
                    cnt_10 <= cnt_10 + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Counter for 1MHz generation
        reg [6:0] cnt_1;
        always @(posedge CLK_in or negedge RST) begin
            if (!RST) begin
                cnt_1 <= 7'b0000000;
            end else begin
                if (cnt_1 == 7'd49) begin
                    cnt_1 <= 7'b0000000;
                end else begin
                    cnt_1 <= cnt_1 + 1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assigning output signals based on counters
        assign CLK_50 = (cnt_50[0] ^ cnt_50[1]);
        assign CLK_10 = (cnt_10 == 4'd4);
        assign CLK_1 = (cnt_1 == 7'd49);
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="alu">
    <description>ALU for a 32-bit MIPS-ISA CPU. Supports various arithmetic and logical operations based on the provided opcode (aluc).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="a" width_description="[31:0]">First operand for the ALU.</input>
        <input id="b" width_description="[31:0]">Second operand for the ALU.</input>
        <input id="aluc" width_description="[5:0]">Control signal specifying the operation to perform.</input>
        <output id="r" width_description="[31:0]">Result of the ALU operation.</output>
        <output id="zero">Indicates if the result is zero.</output>
        <output id="carry">Indicates if there was a carry in the operation.</output>
        <output id="negative">Indicates if the result is negative.</output>
        <output id="overflow">Indicates if there was an overflow in the operation.</output>
        <output id="flag">General flag output, specifically for SLT and SLTU operations.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="ADD" value="6'b100000">Addition operation.</constant>
        <constant name="ADDU" value="6'b100001">Unsigned addition operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SUB" value="6'b100010">Subtraction operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SUBU" value="6'b100011">Unsigned subtraction operation.</constant>
        <constant name="AND" value="6'b100100">Bitwise AND operation.</constant>
        <constant name="OR" value="6'b100101">Bitwise OR operation.</constant>
        <constant name="XOR" value="6'b100110">Bitwise XOR operation.</constant>
        <constant name="NOR" value="6'b100111">Bitwise NOR operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SLT" value="6'b101010">Set less than operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SLTU" value="6'b101011">Unsigned set less than operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SLL" value="6'b000000">Shift left logical operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SRL" value="6'b000010">Shift right logical operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SRA" value="6'b000011">Shift right arithmetic operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SLLV" value="6'b000100">Shift left variable operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SRLV" value="6'b000110">Shift right variable operation.</constant>
        <constant name="SRAV" value="6'b000111">Shift right variable arithmetic operation.</constant>
        <constant name="LUI" value="6'b001111">Load upper immediate operation.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        <assign r[31:0] = res[31:0];>
            Assigns the lower 32 bits of the result to the output 'r'.
        </assign>
        <assign flag = (aluc == SLT || aluc == SLTU) ? 1 : z;>
            Sets the 'flag' output to '1' if the operation is SLT or SLTU, otherwise sets it to high impedance ('z').
        </assign>
        <assign zero = ~(|res);>
            Sets the 'zero' output to '1' if the result is all zeros, otherwise sets it to '0'.
        </assign>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        Always block to determine the result based on the control signal (aluc):
        - Performs addition/subtraction/and/or/xor/nor/slt/sltu/logical shifts (SLL/SRL/SRA) based on the value of aluc.
        - For LUI, concatenates the upper 16 bits of 'a' with 16 zeros.
        - Defaults to high impedance ('z') if aluc doesn't match any defined operation.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="edge_detect">
    <description>Edge detection module for detecting rising and falling edges of input signal a.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="a">Input signal to detect edges.</input>
        <output id="rise">Output signal indicating a rising edge.</output>
        <output id="down">Output signal indicating a falling edge.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                rise <= 0;
                down <= 0;
            end else begin
                if (a && !prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 1;
                    down <= 0;
                end else if (!a && prev_a) begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 1;
                end else begin
                    rise <= 0;
                    down <= 0;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="multi_pipe_4bit">
    <description>Implement a 4-bit unsigned number pipeline multiplier with two levels of registers to store intermediate values and control the multiplication process.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for chip reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="mul_a" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplicand with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="mul_b" width_description="[3:0]">Input signal representing the multiplier with a data width of 4 bits.</input>
        <output id="mul_out" width_description="[7:0]">Product output signal with a data width of 8 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant name="size" value="4">Data width for inputs and outputs.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <implementation>
        Extension of input signals:
        The input signals (mul_a and mul_b) are extended by adding 'size' number of zero bits at the most significant bit positions.
        
        Multiplication operation:
        The module uses a generate block to perform multiplication for each bit position of the multiplier (mul_b) and generate the partial products.
        For each bit position i from 0 to size-1, the partial product is calculated as follows:
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 1, the multiplicand is left-shifted by i positions.
        If the corresponding bit in the multiplier is 0, the partial product is set to 0 ('d0').
        
        Add of partial products:
        The module uses registers to store the intermediate sum values.
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module performs add operations.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the registers are set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the registers are updated with the sum of the corresponding partial products.
        
        Final product calculation:
        On the positive edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the module calculates the final product.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the product output (mul_out) is set to 0.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is high, the product output (mul_out) is updated with the sum of registers.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="asyn_fifo">
    <description>Asynchronous FIFO with configurable depth and width.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="wclk">Write clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rclk">Read clock signal.</input>
        <input id="wrstn">Write reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="rrstn">Read reset signal (active low).</input>
        <input id="winc">Write increment signal.</input>
        <input id="rinc">Read increment signal.</input>
        <input id="wdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Write data input.</input>
        <output id="wfull">Write full signal.</output>
        <output id="rempty">Read empty signal.</output>
        <output id="rdata" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]">Read data output.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="8">Data width of the FIFO.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="16">Depth of the FIFO.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="combinational_logic">
        // Dual-port RAM instantiation
        <instance id="ram" module="dual_port_RAM" width="WIDTH" depth="DEPTH">
            <port id="wclk" connection="wclk"/>
            <port id="wenc" connection="wen"/>
            <port id="waddr" connection="waddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="wdata" connection="wdata"/>
            <port id="rclk" connection="rclk"/>
            <port id="renc" connection="ren"/>
            <port id="raddr" connection="raddr_bin[$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]"/>
            <port id="rdata" connection="rdata"/>
        </instance>
        
        // Write pointer management
        <reg id="waddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= waddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    waddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="wptr" width="4">
            {waddr_bin[3], waddr_bin[3]^waddr_bin[2], waddr_bin[2]^waddr_bin[1], waddr_bin[1]^waddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="wptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (winc && wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= wptr;
                end else if (!wrstn) begin
                    wptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign wen = winc && wrstn;>
        // Read pointer management
        <reg id="raddr_bin" width="$clog2(DEPTH)">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= raddr_bin + 1;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    raddr_bin <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <wire id="rptr" width="4">
            {raddr_bin[3], raddr_bin[3]^raddr_bin[2], raddr_bin[2]^raddr_bin[1], raddr_bin[1]^raddr_bin[0]}
        </wire>
        <reg id="rptr_buff" width="4">
            <always_comb>
                if (rinc && rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= rptr;
                end else if (!rrstn) begin
                    rptr_buff <= 0;
                end
            </always_comb>
        </reg>
        <assign ren = rinc && rrstn;>
        // Full/Empty signal generation
        <assign wfull = (wptr == {~rptr_buff[3], rptr_buff[2:0]});>
        <assign rempty = (rptr == wptr);>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>Asynchronously manages data flow between producer and consumer using dual-port RAM and pointer buffers.</implementation>
</module>

<module id="adder_pipe_64bit">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit ripple carry adder with pipelining stages and registers for synchronization.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="i_en">Enable signal for addition operation.</input>
        <input id="adda" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand A.</input>
        <input id="addb" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit input operand B.</input>
        <output id="result" width_description="[64:0]">65-bit output representing the sum of adda and addb.</output>
        <output id="o_en">Output enable signal indicating the availability of the result.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Pipeline stages
        reg [63:0] adda_reg;
        reg [63:0] addb_reg;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage1;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage2;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage3;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage4;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage5;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage6;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage7;
        reg [63:0] sum_stage8;
        
        // Registers for enabling pipeline stages
        reg en_stage1;
        reg en_stage2;
        reg en_stage3;
        reg en_stage4;
        reg en_stage5;
        reg en_stage6;
        reg en_stage7;
        reg en_stage8;
        
        // Register for output enable signal
        reg o_en_reg;
        
        // Sequential logic for pipeline stages
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                adda_reg <= 64'd0;
                addb_reg <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage1 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage2 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage3 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage4 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage5 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage6 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage7 <= 64'd0;
                sum_stage8 <= 64'd0;
                
                en_stage1 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage2 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage3 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage4 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage5 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage6 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage7 <= 1'b0;
                en_stage8 <= 1'b0;
                
                o_en_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                if (i_en) begin
                    adda_reg <= adda;
                    addb_reg <= addb;
                    
                    en_stage1 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage1) begin
                    sum_stage1 <= adda_reg + addb_reg;
                    
                    en_stage2 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage2) begin
                    sum_stage2 <= sum_stage1;
                    
                    en_stage3 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage3) begin
                    sum_stage3 <= sum_stage2;
                    
                    en_stage4 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage4) begin
                    sum_stage4 <= sum_stage3;
                    
                    en_stage5 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage5) begin
                    sum_stage5 <= sum_stage4;
                    
                    en_stage6 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage6) begin
                    sum_stage6 <= sum_stage5;
                    
                    en_stage7 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage7) begin
                    sum_stage7 <= sum_stage6;
                    
                    en_stage8 <= 1'b1;
                end
                
                if (en_stage8) begin
                    sum_stage8 <= sum_stage7;
                    
                    o_en_reg <= 1'b1;
                end
            end
        end
        
        // Assign outputs
        assign result = {1'b0, sum_stage8};
        assign o_en = o_en_reg;

<module id="pulse_detect">
    <description>Pulse Detection Module: Detects rising edges of data_in and generates a pulse output data_out.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in">One-bit input signal.</input>
        <output id="data_out">Output signal indicating the presence of pulses.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        Always block sensitive to the positive edge of clk and negative edge of rst_n.
        In the reset condition (~rst_n), initialize state to 0 and set data_out to 0.
        For each state, check data_in and transition to the next state based on its value.
        Set data_out to 1 when transitioning from state 3 to state 0, indicating the end of a pulse.
        Otherwise, set data_out to 0.
    </implementation>
</module>

<module id="accu">
    <description>Serial input data accumulation output, input is 8bit data. The valid_in will be set to 1 before the first data comes in. Whenever the module receives 4 input data, the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results and sets the valid_out to be 1 (will last only 1 cycle).</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock input for synchronization.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[7:0]">8-bit input data for addition.</input>
        <input id="valid_in">Input signal indicating readiness for new data.</input>
        <output id="valid_out">Output signal indicating when 4 input data accumulation is reached.</output>
        <output id="data_out" width_description="[9:0]">10-bit output data representing the accumulated sum.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>When valid_in is 1, data_in is a valid input. Accumulate four valid input data_in values and calculate the output data_out by adding these four values together. There is no output when there are fewer than four data_in inputs in the interim. Along with the output data_out, a cycle of valid_out=1 will appear as a signal. The valid_out signal is set to 1 when the data_out outputs 4 received data accumulation results. Otherwise, it is set to 0.</implementation>
</module>

module JC_counter (
    input                clk,
    input                rst_n,
    output reg [63:0]     Q
);

always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
    if (!rst_n) begin
        Q <= 64'b0;
    end
    else begin
        if (Q[0] == 1'b0) begin
            Q <= {1'b1, Q[63:1]};
        end
        else begin
            Q <= {1'b0, Q[63:1]};
        end
    end
end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="JC_counter">
    <description>Implement a 64-bit Johnson counter with specified state transitions.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal to initialize the counter.</input>
        <output id="Q" width_description="[63:0]">64-bit register representing the current count value.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        On every rising edge of the clock signal (clk) or the falling edge of the reset signal (rst_n), the counter value is updated.
        If the reset signal (rst_n) is low, indicating a reset condition, the counter value (Q) is set to 0.
        Otherwise, if the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 0, the counter increments by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 1 at the most significant bit position.
        If the least significant bit (Q[0]) is 1, the counter decrements by shifting the current value (Q[63:1]) to the right and appending a 0 at the most significant bit position.
    </implementation>
</module>

module RAM #(
    parameter WIDTH=6,
    parameter DEPTH=8
) (
    input clk,
    input rst_n,
    input write_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]write_addr,
    input [WIDTH-1:0]write_data,
    input read_en,
    input [$clog2(DEPTH)-1:0]read_addr,
    output reg [WIDTH-1:0]read_data
);

    reg [WIDTH-1:0] RAM [2**WIDTH-1:0];

    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            for (int i = 0; i < 2**WIDTH; i++) begin
                RAM[i] <= 0;
            end
        end
        else if (write_en) begin
            RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
        end
    end

    always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
        if (!rst_n) begin
            read_data <= 0;
        end
        else if (read_en) begin
            read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
        end
        else begin
            read_data <= 0;
        end
    end

endmodule
samples.xml_description:
<module id="RAM">
    <description>Implement a dual-port RAM with a depth of 8 and a bit width of 6 bits, with all data initialized to 000000. It has two groups of ports, respectively for reading data and writing data, and read and write operations can be carried out at the same time. When the read_en signal is 1, the read_data of the corresponding position is read through the read_addr signal and output; When the write_en signal is 1, data is written to the corresponding position through the write_addr signal and write-data signal.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="rst_n">Active-low reset signal. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="write_en">Write enable signal to initiate a write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the write operation.</input>
        <input id="write_data" width_description="[5:0]">Data to be written to the RAM.</input>
        <input id="read_en">Read enable signal to initiate a read operation.</input>
        <input id="read_addr" width_description="[2:0]">Address for the read operation.</input>
        <output id="read_data" width_description="[5:0]">Output signal representing the data read from the RAM.</output>
    </ports>
    <parameter_description>
        <constant id="WIDTH" value="6">Bit width of the RAM.</constant>
        <constant id="DEPTH" value="8">Depth of the RAM.</constant>
    </parameter_description>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="RAM" width_description="[DEPTH-1:0][WIDTH-1:0]" init_value="000000">Memory array for storing data.</reg>
        <reg id="read_data" width_description="[WIDTH-1:0]" init_value="000000">Register to hold the read data.</reg>
    </logic_description>
    <implementation>
        // Write operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset all memory locations to 0
                integer i;
                for (i = 0; i < DEPTH; i = i + 1) begin
                    RAM[i] <= 0;
                end
            end else if (write_en) begin
                // Write data to the specified address
                RAM[write_addr] <= write_data;
            end
        end
        
        // Read operation
        always @(posedge clk or negedge rst_n) begin
            if (!rst_n) begin
                // Reset read data to 0
                read_data <= 0;
            end else if (read_en) begin
                // Read data from the specified address
                read_data <= RAM[read_addr];
            end else begin
                // Clear read data if read_en is not active
                read_data <= 0;
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="right_shifter">
    <description>Right shifter that takes an 8-bit input and outputs the shifted result after each clock cycle.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk">Clock signal used for synchronous operation.</input>
        <input id="d">Input signal to be right-shifted.</input>
        <output id="q" width_description="[7:0]">Output signal representing the result of the right shift operation.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>Initializes the register q to 0. On each rising edge of the clock, it shifts the contents of q to the right by one bit and inserts the new input bit d into the most significant position of the register.</implementation>
    <logic_description type="sequential_logic">
        <reg id="q" width="8" initial_value="0">Register to store the shifted result.</reg>
        <always @(posedge clk)>
            q <= (q >> 1);
            q[7] <= d;
        </always>
    </logic_description>
</module>

samples.xml_description:
<module id="synchronizer">
    <description>Multi-bit MUX-based synchronizer with specific timing requirements.</description>
    <ports>
        <input id="clk_a">Clock signal A used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="clk_b">Clock signal B used for synchronous operations.</input>
        <input id="arstn">Active-low reset signal A. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="brstn">Active-low reset signal B. Defined as 0 for reset and 1 for reset signal inactive.</input>
        <input id="data_in" width_description="[3:0]">Input data signal of size 4 bits.</input>
        <input id="data_en">Input enable signal that controls the selection operation.</input>
        <output id="dataout" width_description="[3:0]">Output data signal of size 4 bits.</output>
    </ports>
    <implementation>
        // Data Register
        reg [3:0] data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                data_reg <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                data_reg <= data_in;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Data Register
        reg en_data_reg;
        always @(posedge clk_a or negedge arstn) begin
            if (!arstn) begin
                en_data_reg <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_data_reg <= data_en;
            end
        end
        
        // Enable Control Registers
        reg en_clap_one, en_clap_two;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                en_clap_one <= 1'b0;
                en_clap_two <= 1'b0;
            end else begin
                en_clap_one <= en_data_reg;
                en_clap_two <= en_clap_one;
            end
        end
        
        // Output Assignment
        reg [3:0] dataout;
        always @(posedge clk_b or negedge brstn) begin
            if (!brstn) begin
                dataout <= 4'b0;
            end else begin
                if (en_clap_two) begin
                    dataout <= data_reg;
                end else begin
                    dataout <= dataout;
                end
            end
        end
    </implementation>
</module>
